questions.question: Describe the basic components and operation of a Linear Congruential Generator (LCG).
keyPoints: Formula: X(n+1) = (a * X(n) + c) mod m
Four parameters: modulus m, multiplier a, increment c, seed X0
Produces a deterministic sequence of pseudorandom numbers from the seed

questions.question: State and explain the Hull-Dobell Theorem for achieving full period in an LCG.
keyPoints: Full period m is achieved if and only if three conditions hold
Condition 1: c and m are coprime, i.e., gcd(c, m) = 1
Condition 2: (a-1) is divisible by every prime factor of m
Condition 3: If 4 divides m, then 4 must also divide (a-1)
Example: m=16, a=5, c=3 satisfies all conditions

questions.question: Explain why LCGs are unsuitable for cryptographic applications. Describe how an attacker can break an LCG.
keyPoints: LCGs are deterministic and predictable once parameters are known
From a few consecutive outputs, attacker can set up linear equations
Using linear algebra or lattice reduction to solve for a, c, m
Once parameters are recovered, all future and past values can be computed

questions.question: Differentiate between mixed and multiplicative LCGs with examples.
keyPoints: Mixed LCG: c != 0, formula X(n+1) = (aX(n) + c) mod m, max period m
Multiplicative LCG (Lehmer RNG): c = 0, formula X(n+1) = (aX(n)) mod m, max period m-1
Example: ANSI C is mixed LCG, MINSTD (a=16807, m=2^31-1) is multiplicative
